findings.description: PERF-BGSTAB-002 async-izes readProcessStartIdentitySync, relaxing FR-BGSTAB-009 AC-2's literal timing ('when the PTY is spawned, then the session stores ... best-effort OS start identity') to 'when the asynchronous capture completes'. After PERF-BGSTAB-002 lands, FR-BGSTAB-009 AC-2 (marked [x] implemented) is temporally no longer exact (identity is stored slightly after spawn). It is reconciled only through the 'best-effort' latitude and PERF-BGSTAB-002 AC-2/Implementation Notes; FR-BGSTAB-009 AC-2 itself is not updated and the relation is labeled 'extends' rather than a timing update. Compatible in practice (best-effort tolerates delayed resolution) but a documented timing shift to a verified AC. Verified code: readProcessStartIdentitySync is synchronous execFileSync/powershell.exe timeout:1000 at processTreeTerminator.ts:328-363 and called on the creation path at SessionManager.ts:2345.

REL-BGSTAB-002's discard lifecycle transition is not recorded in its Change Notes table. The table's last entry is 2026-06-28 'Status -> implemented'; there is no 2026-07-02 discard row despite update_status(REL-BGSTAB-002 -> discarded) (mcp seq 17). The discard IS documented via Status=discarded, the strikethrough header '~~REL-BGSTAB-002 ...~~ [DISCARDED -> see REL-BGSTAB-005]', and a dated Implementation Notes entry ('[2026-07-02] Discarded and superseded by REL-BGSTAB-005 ... re_stated_from AC-1..AC-7'). Provenance and re-statement are otherwise complete, so the gap is a canonical-changelog omission rather than a traceability break.

All five acceptance criteria of PERF-BGSTAB-001 carry a duplicated AC label: '- [ ] AC-1: AC-1: Given ...', '- [ ] AC-2: AC-2: ...', through AC-5 (srs lines 1794-1798). The redundant inner 'AC-N: ' prefix is an isolated authoring defect (other 5 new REQs use a single prefix). validate_spec still reports 0 errors (mcp seq 27), so it is cosmetic, but it is systematic within this REQ.

PERF-BGSTAB-001 AC-3 commits the new per-frame time-budget default to a firm testable range ('within the 6-8ms range'), while eval_input.intent_ambiguities records '6~8ms 는 예시 수치이며 확정값 아님' (6-8ms is an example, not confirmed). Unlike visibleFlushBudgetBytes (a named config key backed by config.schema.ts:150 default 262144), this new per-frame time budget has no named config key and no {{OQ}} placeholder, so a value the analysis flagged as tentative is baked into an AC without an open-question flag. It is verifiable as written (default in [6,8]) so severity is low.

Two soft phrasings in REL-BGSTAB-005: AC-9 uses 'a shutdown runs with a large owned-session count' ('large' is unquantified scene-setting) and AC-11 uses the causal qualifier 'without degraded-timeout cleanup evidence attributable solely to sequential termination scaling' ('solely' is hard to isolate deterministically in a test). Both are mitigated: the load-bearing undecided value is captured in {{OQ-2}} (registered in §6, [NEEDS-USER]), and AC-12 adds concrete bounded-parallel / idempotent-finalization / scaled-timeout regression tests, so the testable core is unaffected.
